Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»What time is the Paris Olympics opening ceremony?

Here is the exact time the Paris Olympics opening ceremony will take place, and when you can expect to see SA’s ‘parade of nations’.

At the beginning of every Olympics, there is always huge interest in the opening ceremony, and it will certainly be interesting to see what has been planned in Paris.

Somewhat strangely, various Olympic events in Paris are already under way, although the opening ceremony will only take place on Friday, 26 July.

The Paris Olympics opening ceremony events are scheduled to begin at 19:30 local time, which is the same as SA time. It will be broadcast on SuperSport’s grandstand channel 201, as well as the dedicated main Olympics channel 203.

One of the most popular parts of the opening ceremony sees all participating countries have grand introductions in front of live audiences and millions of spectators across the globe.

Interestingly, the Paris Olympics will see a slight tweak, and instead of athletes walking down a stage, they’ll be introduced on boats cruising down theRiver Seinein a historic first for a Summer Games.

It should also be noted that South Africa will be the fourth country to appear in the ‘parade of nations’ because SA is known as Afrique du Sud in French.Akani SimbineandCaitlin Rooskrantzwill be the teamflag bearersfor the Paris Olympics, andMpumelelo MhlongoandKathrine Swanepoelfor the Paralympics.

Here’s the full country order for the 2024 Parade of Nations in Paris:

Final additions to the Paris Olympics squad have been made and the names of those added have an asterisk (*) next to them.

AQUATICS (9)Men (3):Pieter Coetze (100m, 200m backstroke), Chad le Clos (100m butterfly), Matthew Sates (100m, 200m butterfly and 200m IM)Women (6):Aimee Canny (200m freestyle), Kaylene Corbett (200m breaststroke), Erin Gallagher (100m butterfly), Rebecca Meder (200m IM), Tatjana Smith (100m, 200m breaststroke), Julia Vincent (diving, 3m springboard)Management:Rocco Meiring, Chanelle van Wyk, Dean Price, Mafata Modutoane

ARCHERY (1)Men:Wian Roux (recurve)Coach/Manager:Patrick Roux

ATHLETICS (34)Men (23):*Kyle Blignaut (shot put), *Edmund du Plessis (800m), Elroy Galant (marathon), *Victor Hogan (discus), Gardeo Isaacs (4x400m), *Cheswill Johnson (long jump), Shaun Maswangani (100m, 200m, 4x100m), Stephen Mokoka (marathon), *Ryan Mphalele (1 500m), Zakithi Nene (400m, 4x400m), Bradley Nkoana (4x100m), Antonie Nortje (4x400m), Lythe Pillay (400m, 4x400m), Francois Prinsloo (discus), *Brian Raats (high jump), Benjamin Richardson (100m, 200m, 4x100m), Akani Simbine (100m, 4x100m), Adrian Swart (4x400m), Tshepo Tshite (1 500m), Wayde van Niekerk (200m), Jovan van Vuuren (long jump), Bayanda Walaza (4x100m), Adriaan Wildschutt (5 000m, 10 000m)Reserve:Sineseipho Dambile (4x100m)Women (11):*Miranda Coetzee (400m), *Mine de Klerk (shot put), Ashley Erasmus (shot put), Marione Fourie (100mH), Zeney Geldenhuys (400mH), Rogail Joseph (400mH), Cian Oldknow (marathon), Prudence Sekgodiso (800m), Gerda Steyn (marathon), *Jo-Ane van Dyk (javelin), Irvette van Zyl (marathon)Management:Jean Verster, Hezekiel Sepeng, Paul Gorries, Maritza Coetzee, Samuel Sepeng, Mike Mbambani, John Smit, Neil Cornelius

BADMINTON (1)Women:Johanita ScholtzCoach/Manager:Dillan Schaap

CANOEING (4)Men (2):Andrew Birkett (kayak sprint), Hamish Lovemore (kayak sprint)Women (2):Tiffany Koch (kayak sprint), Esti Olivier (kayak sprint)Management:Nkosi Mzolo (coach), Janet Simpkins (manager)

CYCLING (8)Men (4):Ryan Gibbons (road), Jean Spies (track), Alan Hatherley (MTB), *Vincent Leygonie (BMX)Women (4):Ashleigh Moolman-Pasio (road), Tiffany Keep (road), Candice Lill (MTB), Miyanda Maseti (BMX)Management:Ciska du Plessis-Austin, Nicole Shuttlewortth, Benedict Moqumo, Barry Austin, *Jean-Pierre Jacobs, Tyler Klumper, Brigitte Mileson, Ryan van der Spuy

EQUESTRIAN (1)Men:Alexander Peternell (eventing)Management:Ingeborg Sanne (manager), Elinor Capostagno (groom)

FENCING (1)Men:Harry Saner (epee)Management:Guennadi Tychler

GOLF (4)Men (2):Christiaan Bezuidenhout, Erik van Rooyen|Women (2):Ashleigh Buhai, Paula RetoCaddies:Alex Gaugert, Jacques Nel, Tanya Paterson, Jeremy SharpeManagement:Dornay Lombard

GYMNASTICS (1)Women:Caitlin Rooskrantz (artistic)Coach/Manager:Ilse Pelser (coach)

HOCKEY (32)Men (16):Daniel Bell, Dayaan Cassiem, Mustapha Cassiem, Calvin Davis, Matthew Guise-Brown, Andrew Hobson, Keenan Horne, Gowan Jones, Ryan Julius, Tevin Kok, Zenani Kraai, Samkelo Mvimbi, Nqobile Ntuli, Bradley Sherwood, Nicholas Spooner, Jacques van TonderReserves:Hendrik Kriek, Nduduza (Peabo) LembetheManagement:Donavin George, Cheslin Chie, Siphesihle Ntuli, Devon van der MerweWomen (16):Taheera Augousti, Quanita Bobbs, Stephanie Botha, Dirkie Chamberlain, Erin Christie, Kayla de Waal, Paris-Gail Isaacs, Marie Antoinette Louw, Ongeziwe Mali, Ntsopa Mokoena, Edith Molikoe, Kristen Paton, Hannah Pearce, Celia Seerane, Anelle van Deventer, Onthatile ZuluReserves: Aphiwe Dimba, Kayla SwartsManagement:Lynn Abrahamse, Giles Bonnet, Marcelle Keet, Wayne Lombard

JUDO (1)Women:Geronay Whitebooi|Manager/coach: WilliamMatike Ndoumbe

ROWING (3)Men (2):John Smith (pair), Christopher Baxter (pair)Coach/Manager:Tiago LoureiroWomen (1):Paige Badenhorst (single skull)Coach/manager:Grant Dodds

RUGBY SEVENS (24)Men (12):Christoffel Grobbelaar, Ryan Oosthuizen, Impi Visser, Zain Davids, Quewin Nortje, Tiaan Pretorius, Shaun Williams, Selvyn Davids, Tristan Leyds, Rosko Specman, Siviwe Soyizwapi, Shilton van WykReserves:Katlego Letebele, Ronald BrownManagement:Philip Snyman, Ghafoer Luckan, Marius Schoeman, Ashley EvertWomen (12):Liske Lategan, Ayanda Malinga,  Kemisetso Baloyi, Zintle Mpupha, Sizophila Solontsi, Mathrin Simmers, Nadine Roos, Elizabeth Janse van Rensburg, Veroeshka Grain, Marlize de Bruin, Maria Tshiremba, Byrhabdre DolfReserves:Shiniqwa Lamprecht. Shona-Leah WestonManagement:Mokgatjane Leseke, Renfred Dazel, Cecil Afrika, Obakeng Molopyane

SKATEBOARDING (3)Men (2):Dallas Oberholzer (park), Brandon Valjalo (street)Women:Boipelo Awuah (street)Manager/coach:Wendy Gila

SPORT CLIMBING (4)Men (2):Joshua Bruyns (speed climbing), Mel Janse van Rensburg (lead boulder)Women (2):Aniya Holder (speed climbing), Lauren Mukheibir (lead  boulder)Management:Dean Bruyns (manager), John-David Muller (official coach)

SURFING (3)Men (2):Matthew McGillivray, Jordy SmithWomen (1):Sarah Ann BaumManagement:Christopher Bond (coach), Rezar De Nicker (coach, manager)

TRIATHLON (3)Men (2):Henri Schoeman, Jamie RiddleWomen (1):Vicky van der MerweManagement:Riana Robertson, Lindsey Parry

WRESTLING (1)Men:Nicholaas de Lange (97kg)Coach/Manager:Jan Roets

IndividualsGold medallists R400 000 and the coach R100 000Silver medallists R200 000 and the coach R 50 000Bronze medallists R75 000 and the coach R 25 000

Relays and team sportsGold medal: R100 000 per player and the coach R100 000Silver medal: R75 000 per player and the coach R75 000Bronze medal: R50 000 per player and the coach R50 000

Boats (2 athletes)Gold medal: R200 000 and the coach R100 000Silver medal: R100 000 and the coach R50 000Bronze medal: R40 000 and the coach R20 000

title: What time is the Paris Olympics opening ceremony?

description: Here is the exact time the Paris Olympics opening ceremony will take place, and when you can expect to see SA's 'parade of nations'.
